annotations.label: Tessellation (tiling)

anchor.snippet: A tessellation or tiling is the covering of a surface

annotations.note: Mathlib has discrete-tiling building blocks (prototiles/placed tiles) but no assembled tessellation covering a surface without gaps/overlaps.

annotations.label: Periodic tiling

anchor.snippet: A periodic tiling has a repeating pattern

annotations.note: No notion of a periodic tiling is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Aperiodic tiling

anchor.snippet: An aperiodic tiling uses a small set of tile shapes

annotations.note: The AperiodicOrder/Delone-set infrastructure underlies aperiodic order but no aperiodic tiling is defined.

annotations.label: Tessellation of space

anchor.snippet: A tessellation of space , also known as a space filling or honeycomb

annotations.note: The dimension-agnostic prototile/placed-tile infra applies to any space but a space-filling honeycomb is not defined.

annotations.label: Fyodorov: 17 isometry groups

anchor.snippet: proved that every periodic tiling of the plane features one of seventeen different groups of isometries

annotations.note: The classification of plane-tiling symmetry into 17 groups is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Regular tessellation (overview)

anchor.snippet: a regular tessellation has both identical

annotations.note: No definition of regular tessellation exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Only three regular tessellation shapes

anchor.snippet: There are only three shapes that can form such regular tessellations

annotations.note: The enumeration of regular tessellations is not formalized.

annotations.label: Eight semi-regular tessellations

anchor.snippet: there are eight types of semi-regular tessellation

annotations.note: The enumeration of semi-regular tessellations is not formalized.

annotations.label: Formal tessellation

anchor.snippet: More formally, a tessellation or tiling is a cover of the Euclidean plane

annotations.note: Mathlib's discrete-tiling tiles model the building blocks but the formal 'cover of the plane by closed sets with disjoint interiors' is not defined.

annotations.label: Prototile

anchor.snippet: Many tessellations are formed from a finite number of prototiles

annotations.note: DiscreteTiling.Prototile formalizes a prototile generally as a tile in a space acted on by a group, with a Protoset giving a finite/indexed family.

annotations.label: Conway criterion

anchor.snippet: The Conway criterion is a sufficient, but not necessary, set of rules

annotations.note: The Conway criterion is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Schläfli symbol

anchor.snippet: He further defined the Schläfli symbol notation

annotations.note: Schläfli symbol notation is not formalized.

annotations.label: Vertex configuration

anchor.snippet: the most common notation is the vertex configuration

annotations.note: Vertex configuration notation is not formalized.

annotations.label: Edge

anchor.snippet: An edge is the intersection between two bordering tiles

annotations.note: Edges of a tiling are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Vertex

anchor.snippet: A vertex is the point of intersection of three or more bordering tiles

annotations.note: Vertices of a tiling are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Isogonal / vertex-transitive tiling

anchor.snippet: an isogonal or vertex-transitive tiling is a tiling where every vertex point is identical

annotations.note: No notion of vertex-transitive tiling is defined.

annotations.label: Fundamental region

anchor.snippet: The fundamental region is a shape such as a rectangle that is repeated

annotations.note: Mathlib's measure-theoretic IsFundamentalDomain/IsAddFundamentalDomain captures a region tiling the space under a group action, though not the tessellation-specific geometric notion.

annotations.label: Edge-to-edge tiling

anchor.snippet: An edge-to-edge tiling is any polygonal tessellation where adjacent tiles only share one full side

annotations.note: Edge-to-edge tilings are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Normal tiling

anchor.snippet: A normal tiling is a tessellation for which every tile is topologically equivalent to a disk

annotations.note: Normal tilings are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Monohedral tiling

anchor.snippet: A monohedral tiling is a tessellation in which all tiles are congruent

annotations.note: Monohedral tilings are not defined, though a single-prototile protoset is conceptually close.

annotations.label: Voderberg tiling

anchor.snippet: the Voderberg tiling has a unit tile that is a nonconvex enneagon

annotations.note: The Voderberg tiling is not formalized.

annotations.label: Regular pentagons cannot tile the plane

anchor.snippet: regular pentagons cannot tile the Euclidean plane

annotations.note: This impossibility result is not formalized.

annotations.label: Isohedral tiling

anchor.snippet: An isohedral tiling is a special variation of a monohedral tiling

annotations.note: Isohedral (tile-transitive) tilings are not defined.

annotations.label: Anisohedral tiling

anchor.snippet: then the prototile is called anisohedral and forms anisohedral tilings

annotations.note: Anisohedral tilings are not defined.

annotations.label: Regular tessellation

anchor.snippet: A regular tessellation is a highly symmetric , edge-to-edge tiling made up of regular polygons

annotations.note: Regular tessellations are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Only three regular tessellations

anchor.snippet: There are only three regular tessellations

annotations.note: The enumeration of the three regular tessellations is not formalized.

annotations.label: Semi-regular (Archimedean) tessellation

anchor.snippet: A semi-regular (or Archimedean) tessellation uses more than one type of regular polygon

annotations.note: Semi-regular tessellations are not defined.

annotations.label: Eight semi-regular tilings

anchor.snippet: There are eight semi-regular tilings

annotations.note: The enumeration of semi-regular tilings is not formalized.

annotations.label: Pythagorean tilings

anchor.snippet: the family of Pythagorean tilings , tessellations that use two (parameterised) sizes of square

annotations.note: Pythagorean tilings are not formalized.

annotations.label: Edge tessellation

anchor.snippet: An edge tessellation is one in which each tile can be reflected over an edge

annotations.note: Edge tessellations are not defined.

annotations.label: 17 wallpaper groups

anchor.snippet: can be categorized by wallpaper groups , of which 17 exist

annotations.note: The classification of the 17 wallpaper groups is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Seven frieze groups

anchor.snippet: may be categorized by the seven frieze groups

annotations.note: The classification of the seven frieze groups is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Penrose tilings

anchor.snippet: Penrose tilings , which use two different quadrilateral prototiles

annotations.note: Penrose tilings are not formalized (the 'Penrose' hits are unrelated diagram widgets).

annotations.label: Aperiodic tilings

anchor.snippet: They belong to a general class of aperiodic tilings

annotations.note: The AperiodicOrder/Delone-set framework is foundational to aperiodic order but aperiodic tilings themselves are not defined.

annotations.label: Substitution tiling

anchor.snippet: The recursive process of substitution tiling is a method of generating aperiodic tilings

annotations.note: Substitution tilings are not formalized.

annotations.label: Rep-tiles

anchor.snippet: One class that can be generated in this way is the rep-tiles

annotations.note: Rep-tiles are not formalized.

annotations.label: Pinwheel tilings

anchor.snippet: Pinwheel tilings are non-periodic, using a rep-tile construction

annotations.note: Pinwheel tilings are not formalized.

annotations.label: Wang tiles

anchor.snippet: Wang tiles are squares coloured on each edge

annotations.note: Wang tiles are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Wang tiling undecidability

anchor.snippet: the problem of deciding whether a Wang domino set can tile the plane is also undecidable

annotations.note: The undecidability of the Wang domino problem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Truchet tiles

anchor.snippet: Truchet tiles are square tiles decorated with patterns

annotations.note: Truchet tiles are not formalized.

annotations.label: Einstein tile

anchor.snippet: An einstein tile is a single shape that forces aperiodic tiling

annotations.note: The einstein (aperiodic monotile) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Four colour theorem

anchor.snippet: The four colour theorem states that for every tessellation of a normal Euclidean plane

annotations.note: The four colour theorem is not proved in Mathlib (it appears only in the docs/100.yaml tracking list).

annotations.label: Seven colours for symmetry

anchor.snippet: as many as seven colours may be needed

annotations.note: This colouring result for symmetric tilings is not formalized.

annotations.label: Triangles and quadrilaterals tile

anchor.snippet: Any triangle or quadrilateral (even non-convex ) can be used as a prototile

annotations.note: The fact that any triangle/quadrilateral tiles the plane is not formalized.

annotations.label: Arbitrary quadrilateral tessellation

anchor.snippet: Copies of an arbitrary quadrilateral can form a tessellation with translational symmetry

annotations.note: This tessellation result is not formalized.

annotations.label: Convex N-gon tilings exist

anchor.snippet: tilings exist with convex N -gons for N equal to 3, 4, 5, and 6

annotations.note: Existence of convex N-gon tilings is not formalized.

annotations.label: Polyominoes

anchor.snippet: Polyominoes are examples of tiles that are either convex of non-convex

annotations.note: Polyominoes are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Voronoi / Dirichlet tilings

anchor.snippet: Voronoi or Dirichlet tilings are tessellations where each tile is defined as the set of points closest

annotations.note: Voronoi/Dirichlet tilings are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Voronoi cell is convex polygon

anchor.snippet: The Voronoi cell for each defining point is a convex polygon

annotations.note: Convexity of Voronoi cells is not formalized.

annotations.label: Delaunay triangulation

anchor.snippet: The Delaunay triangulation is a tessellation that is the dual graph of a Voronoi tessellation

annotations.note: Delaunay triangulations are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Delaunay maximises minimum angle

anchor.snippet: Delaunay triangulations maximize the minimum of the angles formed by the edges

annotations.note: The max-min-angle property of Delaunay triangulations is not formalized.

annotations.label: Polyhedra filling 3D space

anchor.snippet: Certain polyhedra can be stacked in a regular crystal pattern to fill (or tile) three-dimensional space

annotations.note: Space-filling polyhedra results are not formalized.

annotations.label: Plesiohedron

anchor.snippet: Any polyhedron that fits this criterion is known as a plesiohedron

annotations.note: Plesiohedra are not defined.

annotations.label: Honeycomb

anchor.snippet: Tessellations in three or more dimensions are called honeycombs

annotations.note: Honeycombs (higher-dimensional tessellations) are not defined.

annotations.label: One regular 3D honeycomb

anchor.snippet: In three dimensions there is just one regular honeycomb

annotations.note: The uniqueness of the regular 3D honeycomb is not formalized.

annotations.label: One quasiregular 3D honeycomb

anchor.snippet: there is just one quasiregular

annotations.note: The uniqueness of the quasiregular 3D honeycomb is not formalized.

annotations.label: Schmitt-Conway biprism

anchor.snippet: The Schmitt-Conway biprism is a convex polyhedron with the property of tiling space only aperiodically

annotations.note: The Schmitt-Conway biprism is not formalized.

annotations.label: Schwarz triangle

anchor.snippet: A Schwarz triangle is a spherical triangle that can be used to tile a sphere

annotations.note: Schwarz triangles are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Uniform tiling in hyperbolic plane

anchor.snippet: A uniform tiling in the hyperbolic plane

annotations.note: Uniform hyperbolic tilings are not formalized.

annotations.label: Uniform honeycomb in hyperbolic space

anchor.snippet: A uniform honeycomb in hyperbolic space is a uniform tessellation of uniform polyhedral cells

annotations.note: Uniform hyperbolic honeycombs are not formalized.

annotations.label: Nine Coxeter honeycomb families

anchor.snippet: there are nine Coxeter group families of compact convex uniform honeycombs

annotations.note: Mathlib has abstract Coxeter groups but not the classification of compact convex uniform hyperbolic honeycombs.

annotations.label: Gilbert tessellation

anchor.snippet: The Gilbert tessellation is a mathematical model for the formation of mudcracks

annotations.note: The Gilbert tessellation model is not formalized.

annotations.label: Squaring the square

anchor.snippet: Squaring the square is the problem of tiling an integral square

annotations.note: Squaring the square is not formalized.

annotations.label: Squaring the plane (Henle)

anchor.snippet: James and Frederick Henle proved that this was possible

annotations.note: The Henle squaring-the-plane result is not formalized.

annotations.label: Triangular tiling

anchor.snippet: Triangular tiling , one of the three regular tilings of the plane

annotations.note: The triangular tiling is not formalized.

annotations.label: Snub hexagonal tiling

anchor.snippet: Snub hexagonal tiling , a semiregular tiling of the plane

annotations.note: The snub hexagonal tiling is not formalized.

annotations.label: Floret pentagonal tiling

anchor.snippet: Floret pentagonal tiling , dual to a semiregular tiling

annotations.note: The floret pentagonal tiling is not formalized.

annotations.label: Voderberg tiling (spiral)

anchor.snippet: The Voderberg tiling , a spiral, monohedral tiling made of enneagons

annotations.note: The Voderberg spiral tiling is not formalized.

annotations.label: Alternated octagonal tiling

anchor.snippet: Alternated octagonal or tritetragonal tiling is a uniform tiling of the hyperbolic plane

annotations.note: The alternated octagonal hyperbolic tiling is not formalized.
